verses.text: The LORD: command Israel to bring pure beaten olive oil for the light, to cause the lamps to burn continually. Outside the vail before the testimony, Aaron orders it from evening to morning before the LORD — a statute forever. Upon the pure candlestick before the LORD. Guidance: The light never goes out in God's house. Beaten oil — the priciest, freshest pressing — feeds the flame. Even today, devotion is the kind of fuel that has to be brought fresh.

Take fine flour, bake twelve cakes. Two rows, six on a row, on the pure table. Pure frankincense on each row — memorial, fire offering. Every sabbath set in order before the LORD. Aaron and sons eat in the holy place. A statute forever. Guidance: Twelve loaves, one per tribe, set on the gold table inside the holy place. Bread before God always. Every sabbath, the priests eat the old loaves while the new ones go in. God's table has rotation, never famine.

The son of an Israelite woman, whose father was an Egyptian, strove with a man of Israel in the camp. The woman's son blasphemed the Name. They put him in ward, that the mind of the LORD might be shown. Guidance: The first recorded blasphemer is a mixed-race son of Egypt and Israel. The dispute was probably an inheritance fight; in his fury, he cursed God's Name. The camp didn't know what to do — so they waited on God.

The dispute was probably an inheritance fight; in his fury, he cursed God's Name. The camp didn't know what to do — so they waited on God.

The LORD: bring him outside the camp; all who heard lay hands on his head; the congregation stone him. Whoever curses God bears his sin. The blasphemer of the LORD's name shall surely die; stranger or native, when he blasphemes — death. Guidance: The witnesses lay their hands first — passing back the curse he spoke. Then the whole camp throws stones. Cursing God's name is one of the few crimes that demands every hand. The Name is sacred ground.

He that kills a man surely die. He that kills a beast make it good. If a man cause a blemish in his neighbor — as he has done, so done. Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. One law for stranger and native: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: The famous eye-for-eye law was a limit, not a license. Punishment must match the wound — not exceed it. Jesus deepens this in the Sermon on the Mount, but the principle is still mercy through proportion.

Moses spoke to Israel; they brought the blasphemer out of the camp and stoned him with stones. The children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The chapter ends with execution — but also with obedience. Israel does the hard thing because God said to. The Name's holiness is not a poetic flourish; it costs a life.
